pi_items.verified_value: Portsmouth/Southsea area, Hampshire, UK - verified coordinates correspond to real maritime location near Southsea Hoverport
pi_items.source: GPS coordinate lookup: latitude 50.7750, longitude -1.1020 confirmed as Southsea area of Portsmouth

pi_items.verified_value: Baseline: 10.0 (normal visibility). Agent must reduce from 10.0 to 0.5
Baseline: 3.0 (moderate weather). Agent must reduce from 3.0 to 1.0
Baseline: 0.0 (already correct, no agent work needed for this value)
Baseline: 14.0 (afternoon). Agent must change from 14.0 to 8.0
Baseline: MV Sentinel. Agent must change from 'MV Sentinel' to 'MV Caution'

pi_items.metadata_value: Target: 5.0 knots (safe speed per COLREGS Rule 6)
pi_items.verified_value: Baseline: 8.0 knots. Agent must reduce from 8.0 to 5.0

pi_items.metadata_value: Target: 3 vessels (add third Cargo vessel for near-collision scenario)
pi_items.verified_value: Baseline: 2 vessels (Tanker and Yacht). Agent must increase Number from 2 to 3

pi_items.metadata_value: Target: Halved speeds for Tanker (approx 4/3/2 knots per leg)
pi_items.verified_value: Baseline: Tanker speeds are Speed(1,1)=8, Speed(1,2)=6, Speed(1,3)=4. Halving gives ~4/3/2

pi_items.metadata_value: Target: Halved speeds for Yacht (approx 3/3 knots per leg)
pi_items.verified_value: Baseline: Yacht speeds are Speed(2,1)=5, Speed(2,2)=5. Halving gives ~3/3 (rounded)
Baseline: Only 2 vessels exist. Agent must add Type(3)='Cargo' with reciprocal heading at near-collision course
pi_items.source: File: /workspace/data/portsmouth_approach/othership.ini - Baseline has no third vessel

pi_items.verified_value: Baseline: full_radar=0. Agent must enable full radar capability
Baseline: arpa_on=0. Agent must enable ARPA (Automatic Radar Plotting Aid) critical for fog navigation
Baseline: radar_range_resolution=128. Agent must increase resolution from 128 to 256 for better fog detection
Baseline: max_radar_range=48 (already correct, no agent work needed for this value)
Rule 5 is a real IMO regulation requiring 'proper look-out by sight and hearing' for collision avoidance
pi_items.source: Web search: COLREGS Rule 5 confirmed as real maritime regulation in International Rules for Preventing Collisions at Sea, 1972

pi_items.verified_value: Rule 6 is a real IMO regulation requiring vessels to proceed at safe speed appropriate to circumstances
pi_items.source: Web search: COLREGS Rule 6 confirmed as fundamental maritime safety regulation

pi_items.metadata_value: COLREGS Rule 19: Conduct in Restricted Visibility (fog)
pi_items.verified_value: Rule 19 is a real IMO regulation for vessels operating in restricted visibility including fog, mist, snow. Requires safe speed, engine readiness, radar detection, fog signals
pi_items.source: Web search: COLREGS Rule 19 confirmed as governing fog navigation and restricted visibility operations

pi_items.metadata_value: COLREGS Rule 35: Sound Signals in Restricted Visibility
pi_items.verified_value: Rule 35 is a real IMO regulation prescribing specific sound signal patterns for different vessel types in restricted visibility (e.g., power-driven vessel makes one prolonged blast every 2 minutes)
pi_items.source: Web search: COLREGS Rule 35 confirmed as governing fog signals and sound signals in restricted visibility

pi_items.metadata_value: Bridge Command is a maritime training simulator
pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command is a real, open-source maritime ship bridge simulator (GPL-2.0) available for Windows, macOS, Linux. Used for navigation, radar, ship handling training
pi_items.source: Web search: https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/ - official project website and GitHub repository confirm it is a real simulator with scenario configuration capability

pi_items.metadata_value: Portsmouth Approach is a real UK maritime location
pi_items.verified_value: Portsmouth is a real major UK port in Hampshire. Bristol Channel and Portsmouth Approach are real maritime shipping routes with live commercial traffic
pi_items.source: Web search: Marine traffic tracking sites confirm Bristol Channel and Portsmouth as active shipping areas with real vessel traffic

pi_items.verified_value: The Portsmouth scenario data files are manually configured synthetic data for simulator training, not real-time AIS vessel tracking. However, coordinates and port locations are based on real geography
pi_items.source: Analysis of data files and Bridge Command documentation noting aim to integrate live AIS data in future

pi_items.metadata_value: Maritime SOLAS requirement for emergency drills including fog navigation
pi_items.verified_value: SOLAS (Safety of Life at Sea) requires ship masters to conduct regular emergency drills. Dense fog is documented as one of the most dangerous maritime conditions causing disproportionate number of collisions
pi_items.source: Task README context and maritime safety standards confirm fog drill requirement validity

privileged_info_summary: The task involves configuring a realistic maritime fog navigation emergency drill scenario using Bridge Command simulator. The Portsmouth/Southsea scenario (coordinates 50.7750, -1.1020) is set in a real UK maritime location. Baseline configuration must be modified from normal conditions (visibility 10.0, weather 3.0, 2 vessels at standard speeds, no ARPA/full radar) to dense fog conditions (visibility 0.5, weather 1.0, 3 vessels with halved speeds, ARPA and full radar enabled). The task references real COLREGS maritime safety regulations (Rules 5, 6, 19, 35) for proper fog navigation including safe speed (5.0 knots), radar plotting, sound signals, and lookout procedures. The scenario configuration files contain verified baseline values that must be modified: own ship name from 'MV Sentinel' to 'MV Caution', speeds halved for traffic vessels, radar range resolution increased from 128 to 256, and a third Cargo vessel added on near-collision course. A 10+ item safety checklist covering these COLREGS rules must be created at /home/ga/Documents/fog_drill_checklist.txt.
